name: BSI TR-03182 — Email Authentication (Germany)
description: German Federal Office for Information Security email-authentication conformance profile (SPF, DKIM, DMARC with strict alignment, dual RSA+Ed25519 signing, DNSSEC, STARTTLS). 'compliant' requires all externally-verifiable required_signals. Verification-side and organisational requirements (Authentication-Results, oversigning, report sending/receiving, security concept, GDPR processing) are attestation-only and out of automated scope.
attestation_only: SPF/DKIM/DMARC verification with DNSSEC-validating resolver + Authentication-Results header (TR-03182-02/05/07)
DKIM consistent signing, oversigning, no trace-header signing (TR-03182-04)
Security concept, data protection, mandatory reporting, transparency (TR-03182-12..15)

dns_hygiene.name: DNS hygiene & resilience
dns_hygiene.recommendation: Every MX must resolve and must not be a CNAME; publish CAA; keep forward-confirmed reverse DNS on all MX addresses; use at least two nameservers from more than one provider; leave no dangling CNAME on mta-sts/autodiscover/autoconfig.

reputation.name: Reputation (DNSBL)
reputation.recommendation: Neither the domain nor any MX address should be listed on a public blocklist. A listing indicates active abuse or compromise, not misconfiguration.

subdomains.name: Subdomain coverage
subdomains.recommendation: Every live subdomain must be covered by an enforcing DMARC policy, inherited via sp= or published locally; no subdomain record may be weaker than the apex sp=.

scoring_note: v0.6.0: dns_hygiene, reputation and subdomains are SEE-Monitor controls, not clauses of this standard. They are weighted so that infrastructure failures affect the score, but they are deliberately absent from required_signals/very_strong_requirements, which mirror the published document only.
